items.title: 'Right fit': Ken Hinkley announced as inaugural Tasmania Devils coach

children.content: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

gallery.altText: Ken Hinkley, wearing a Tasmania Devils AFL jacket, fronts the microphones alongside Grant O'Brien and Brendan Gale.

thumbnail.altText: Ken Hinkley, wearing a Tasmania Devils AFL jacket, fronts the microphones alongside Grant O'Brien and Brendan Gale.

items.title: Hobart Private doctor who does gender-affirming surgery worried over imminent sale to Catholic provider

image.altText: Exterior signage at Hobart Private Hospital.

children.content: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

items.title: Paramedics told not to take complex cardiac patients to Mersey hospital

children.content: Paramedics have been told to transport complex cardiac patients in Tasmania's north-west to the Launceston General Hospital rather than the local Mersey Community Hospital.

image.altText: A hospital exterior

items.title: Despite barely any population growth, Tasmania doesn't have enough houses

children.content: Despite having the slowest growing population in the country, Tasmania is still battling rising homelessness and an ever-tightening rental market.

image.altText: A house model and a key.

items.title: For some in Australia, hope is fading and anger is rising

children.content: Sulaiman Zandinan says Australia is amazing. Wendy Newman says people in the bush are "not country bumpkins". The ABC is inviting you to have Your Say about the issues, challenges and opportunities that shape your life in 2026.

cta.title: What matters to you? The ABC wants to know

gallery.altText: A man with curly hair smiles while holding a sports whistle looking away

thumbnail.altText: A man with curly hair smiles while holding a sports whistle looking away

items.title: Lesbian couple told they are no longer the same family by fertility clinic

children.content: Queensland Health advice, given to fertility providers to help navigate the state's assisted reproductive technology legislation, has had a "devastating" impact on some same-sex couples trying to have more children.

image.altText: Two women kissing the cheeks of a young girl at a kitchen table.

items.title: Alone, robbed and in pain: A man finds himself on Sydney's streets

children.content: People with lived experience of homelessness say the issue is deepening as housing stock remains out of reach and community providers are left struggling to cope with demand.

image.altText: Tian in a grey beanie and yellow jacket in dark

items.title: Abbott amplifies Hanson problem for Liberals

children.content: Pauline Hanson's grievance politics is a challenge for Labor, but a splitting headache for the Liberals — compounded by former prime minister Tony Abbott.

image.altText: A close-up of Tony Abbott speaking at an outdoor event.

images.altText: The stage and coffin of Derryn Hinch during a State Funeral Service for Derryn Hinch

children.content: Derryn Hinch, the outspoken — and at times controversial — broadcaster and former senator, is being farewelled at a state funeral service in Melbourne after his death earlier this month, aged 82.

items.title: Derryn Hinch's brother remembers 'colourful, constantly changing' life

updates.title: Decades-long assistant pays tribute to Hinch

items.title: Why the Tate brothers were arrested and what they are charged with

children.content: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami after UK prosecutors brought further rape and sex trafficking charges against them. Here's what we know about the arrests and extradition request.

image.altText: Andrew Tate, left, watches his brother Tristan speak.

items.title: Australia is making humanoid robots in the midst of its AI reckoning

children.content: Australia this week made an important step forward in grappling with the AI revolution. Meanwhile, in Melbourne a company is making humanoid robots.

image.altText: Andromeda's Abi robot

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: War and AI worries drag down Wall Street, ASX edges higher, oil jumps

children.content: The ASX has edged higher despite Wall Street's retreat on Friday as investors continued to bail out on the AI sector. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: L7 bassist Jennifer Finch dies days after revealing cancer diagnosis

children.content: Grunge trailblazers L7 honour their "beloved bandmate, friend and fellow troublemaker", who died just five days after revealing she had been diagnosed with aggressive brain cancer.

image.altText: Bass-playing white woman in her 40s smiles as she performs live on stage in front of drum kit and backing sheet

children.content: From Mexican wrestling to Indian slums, catch up on every week seven film from Race Around the World.

items.title: Why learning to switch off is important for work-life balance

children.content: When being across trends in your industry feels like part of your job it can feel like work never ends. Here's how to protect your free time.

image.altText: A photo taken from above of a woman with a cat on couch, doing work on a tablet.

items.title: Try this week's history quiz (we made it an easy one)

children.content: Consider yourself a history buff? Test your smarts on the first Moon landing, John Howard and the Riot Act.

items.title: Premier says 'witch' billboard dehumanises women, drives violence

children.content: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan says a controversial billboard seen around Melbourne again is corrosive and driving disrespect for women.

gallery.altText: A billboard featuring a sexist message about the Victorian premier

items.title: Pauline Hanson 'not backing away' from controversial far-right interview

children.content: The One Nation leader was a guest on the podcast of controversial far-right UK figure Tommy Robinson, where she lamented the end of the White Australia policy.

image.altText: A woman with red hair wearing a black top.

items.title: Holocaust museum staff and volunteers targeted by hateful comments

children.content: Melbourne Holocaust Museum has had to step up security since 2020 following a rise in incidents that have seen threatening voice messages and hateful comments left online.

image.altText: Breann Fellon standing next to a stain glass window

items.title: Home Affairs asks DV survivors to prove abuser relationship 'genuine'

children.content: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

gallery.altText: An anonymous woman hidden by a solar flare.

thumbnail.altText: An anonymous woman hidden by a solar flare.

items.title: Uranium export deal sparks talk of opportunities for Broken Hill

children.content: Australia's trade deal to supply India with unenriched uranium sparks conversations about potential new mining opportunities in Broken Hill.

image.altText: An aerial view of a mine in remote Australia.

items.title: New AI-powered alert system to warn drivers of approaching wildlife

children.content: An estimated 10 million animals are killed on Australian roads each year. It's hoped a new artificial intelligence-powered alert system being trialled on the Monaro Highway near Canberra will reduce the toll.

image.altText: A wildlife sign with a digital panel at the bottom.

items.title: Decade of work helps remote island's native wildlife 'return to 1616'

children.content: Researchers were unafraid to get their hands "dirty" in an ambitious bid to restore WA's largest island to its pre-colonial state.

items.title: Banners at Melbourne game 'disgraceful', Football Victoria says

children.content: A professional football club in Melbourne's north faces calls to ban hardline supporters who brandished sexist and "ethnically derogatory" banners at a match on Saturday.

image.altText: Supporters of the Preston Lions hold up a banner reading "Anti-Hellass".

items.title: Road plan for new airport 'flawed and shortsighted', critics say

children.content: Some transport planners say the design of a major Western Sydney road upgrade is "nonsensical" and is at odds with the government's commitment to public transport and new housing.

items.title: Rules to tighten on AI use in decision-making by government departments

children.content: Agencies like Centrelink and Services Australia will have to follow a framework to ensure decision-making is "fair and accurate" if they use AI.

items.title: Police release suspect's image after alleged kidnapping attempt

children.content: Investigators say a 33-year-old woman was walking home from work in Warrnambool, south-west Victoria, when a woman tried to push her into a car.

items.title: Five-year wait for much-needed housing in WA's holiday hotspot

children.content: A housing charity says bureaucratic delays have held up its attempt to build some small homes in Margaret River.
